candidate_problems.text: How can 'internally load-bearing distinctions' be operationalized and measured non-circularly? The conjecture hinges on non-interchangeability under substitution, but it remains open how to define the relevant interventions, compensation costs, scope across tasks, and thresholds for 'broad success' without smuggling in prior semantic judgments.

Is load-bearing inferential role sufficient for genuine meaning, or only for system-internal functional organization? A major unresolved tension is whether a tightly coupled web of constraints can generate semantics without any further relation to the world, normativity, or truth-conditions, especially in cases of coherent but systematically misaligned or purely simulated systems.

What system architectures and learning dynamics cause merely correlational patterns to become globally constraint-bearing concepts? The conjecture identifies a criterion for meaning, but leaves open the mechanism: which feedback loops, multimodal couplings, action channels, memory structures, or training regimes actually produce representations that become indispensable across contexts rather than remaining superficial labels.
